title: Port types from pi-messenger/lib.ts
desc: Create types.ts with FileReservation, ReservationConflict, AgentEntry, ClaimEntry, CompletionEntry, and utility functions like pathMatchesReservation.
acceptance: pathMatchesReservation works for files and folders

title: Port withSwarmLock from pi-messenger/store.ts
desc: Create lock.ts with file-based mutex. Uses O_EXCL for atomic lock creation, PID for stale detection.
acceptance: Stale locks (>10s, dead PID) are cleaned

title: Create reservations.ts core logic
desc: Port getConflicts, reserve, release functions. Adapt isProcessAlive to isSessionAlive (placeholder for now).
acceptance: reserve() adds patterns to agent entry
Mesh entries stored as JSON files

title: Create claims.ts for task claiming
desc: Port claimTask, unclaimTask, completeTask with swarm lock protection.
acceptance: claimTask fails if agent has existing claim

title: Create tools.ts with OpenClaw tool wrappers
desc: Wrap core functions as coordination_reserve, coordination_release, coordination_mesh, coordination_conflicts, coordination_claim, coordination_unclaim, coordination_complete.
acceptance: Each tool has proper params interface

title: Create index.ts extension entry point
desc: Export all types, functions, and tools. Create extensionManifest for OpenClaw discovery.

title: Implement isSessionAlive with gateway API
desc: Replace placeholder isSessionAlive() with actual gateway session check. Call 'openclaw sessions list --json' or use HTTP API.

title: Add TypeScript build and verify compilation
desc: Run tsc, fix any type errors, ensure dist/ is generated correctly.

title: Write unit tests for lock.ts
desc: Test withSwarmLock concurrency, stale lock cleanup, tryAcquireLock behavior.

title: Write unit tests for reservations.ts
desc: Test reserve/release/getConflicts with mock session checks.
acceptance: Pattern matching works for files and folders

title: Write unit tests for claims.ts
desc: Test claimTask/unclaimTask/completeTask race conditions and error cases.

title: Integration test: Two agents reserving same file
desc: Spawn two subagents, have both try to reserve 'src/api/'. Verify first succeeds, second gets conflict.
acceptance: After first releases, second can reserve

title: Integration test: Task claiming race
desc: Spawn two subagents racing to claim same task. Verify exactly one succeeds.

title: Document extension usage in README
desc: Write README.md explaining how to use the coordination tools, with examples.

title: Create CLI interface
desc: Create bin/coord.ts with yargs or commander. Set up package.json bin entry. Support subcommands: reserve, release, mesh, conflicts.

title: Implement reserve command
desc: ./bin/coord reserve --patterns 'src/api/' --agent 'coder' --reason 'work'. Calls addReservations(), outputs JSON result.

title: Implement release command
desc: ./bin/coord release --patterns 'src/api/' --agent 'coder'. Calls removeReservations(), outputs JSON result.

title: Implement mesh command
desc: ./bin/coord mesh. Shows all active agents and their reservations as JSON.

title: Implement conflicts command
desc: ./bin/coord conflicts --file 'src/api/auth.ts'. Checks if file conflicts with any reservation.

title: Test all CLI commands
desc: Manual and scripted tests of all CLI commands. Verify JSON parsing works.
